stem: A delivery truck travels at a constant speed of 55 miles per hour. How many feet does the truck travel in 6 minutes? (1 mile = 5,280 feet)
explanation:
Step 1: Convert 6 minutes to hours, since the speed is given in miles per hour. $$6 \text{ min} \times \frac{1 \text{ hr}}{60 \text{ min}} = \frac{6}{60} = \frac{1}{10} \text{ hr}$$

Step 2: Find the distance in miles. $$55 \frac{\text{mi}}{\text{hr}} \times \frac{1}{10} \text{ hr} = 5.5 \text{ miles}$$

Step 3: Convert miles to feet. $$5.5 \times 5{,}280 = 29{,}040 \text{ feet}$$

The truck travels 29,040 feet in 6 minutes.

distractorRationale.A: Ignored unit conversion entirely — the student skips both required conversions (minutes to hours and miles to feet) and simply multiplies the speed by the raw number of minutes: $55 \times 6 = 330$. No unit conversions are performed.
distractorRationale.B: Stopped at intermediate value — the student correctly converts 6 minutes to $\frac{1}{10}$ hour and multiplies by 55 mph to get 5.5 miles, but stops there and reports 5.5 without converting miles to feet.
distractorRationale.C: Wrong unit conversion factor — the student correctly finds 5.5 miles but converts to yards using 1 mile = 1,760 yards, then reports the result as if it were feet. Wrong calculation: $5.5 \times 1{,}760 = 9{,}680$.
